headline: USA RARE EARTH SOARS: $2.8 Billion Serra Verde Acquisition Creates Non-Asian Rare Earth Supply Chain
snippet: USA Rare Earth (NASDAQ: USAR) agreed to acquire Brazil's Serra Verde Group for $2.8 billion in cash and stock, gaining the Pela Ema mine producing all four critical magnetic rare earths — Nd, Pr, Tb, Dy — at 6,400 mtpa TREO. The deal includes a 15-year U.S. government-backed offtake for 100% of Phase 1 output, with management projecting $300–650M run-rate EBITDA by end-2027.

headline: 408 METRES of Rare Earth Ore: Neotech Metals Drills Gallium-Rich Intercept at Ontario's Hecla-Kilmer
snippet: Neotech Metals (CSE: NTMC) intersected 408.4m of 0.53% TREO, 0.13% Nb₂O₅ and 21.5 g/t Ga₂O₃ at the Hecla-Kilmer project in Ontario, with a broader 755m continuous interval averaging 0.38% TREO. The project sits 20 km from active rail and a 180 MW hydro station; a maiden resource estimate is targeted for later in 2026.

headline: CHINA REVERSES COURSE: 7-Month BHP Iron Ore Ban Lifted After CEO Flies to Beijing — Futures Tumble 1%
snippet: China's state iron ore buyer CMRG released 8.69 million tonnes of piled-up BHP portside inventory on April 28, ending a seven-month ban that had squeezed Chinese steel mill supply. The resolution followed BHP CEO Mike Henry's visit to China Baowu's chairman in Shanghai; Dalian iron ore futures fell 1.02% on the immediate supply overhang.

headline: HIGH GRADE HITS Keep Coming: STLLR Gold Drills 6.03 g/t Au Over 22.25m at Ontario's Jonpol Deposit
snippet: STLLR Gold (TSX: STLR) reported 6.03 g/t Au over 22.25m — including 12.67 g/t over 9.25m — from the western strike extension of the Jonpol Deposit at the Tower Gold Project in the Timmins Camp, Ontario. Results exceed current average open-pit grades in the MRE and support potential expansion of the open-pit strike length to 1,200 metres.

headline: SILVER SNAPS BACK Above $76 After Iran Submits Strait of Hormuz Ceasefire Proposal
snippet: Silver bounced back above $76/oz on April 27 after Iran submitted a new proposal aimed at reopening the Strait of Hormuz, reversing earlier session losses tied to stalled US-Iran negotiations. Silver remains near multi-year highs as the market tracks toward its projected sixth consecutive annual supply deficit.

headline: 30.7m AT 2.49% CuEq: GreenLight Metals Extends Copper-Gold VMS Discovery Down-Dip in Wisconsin
snippet: GreenLight Metals (TSXV: GRL) hit 30.7m of 2.49% CuEq including a 14.9m high-grade core of 3.65% CuEq at the Bend VMS deposit in Taylor County, Wisconsin — extending copper-gold-telluride mineralization down-dip in hole B26-007. Every 2026 drill hole has encountered significant sulfide mineralization; three additional holes with visual intercepts are awaiting assays.

headline: ROBOT MINERS: Tesla Vet Reopens Utah Copper Mine as World's First Fully Autonomous Operation — Costs Halved
snippet: Mariana Minerals, led by Tesla veteran Turner Caldwell, has restarted the Copper One mine near Moab as the world's first fully autonomous copper mine, deploying robotic haul trucks and autonomous drills to cut mining costs 50% and refining costs 30%. With copper at $13,000/t, the company targets 50,000 tonnes of refined copper annually by 2030.

headline: GOLD AS MONETARY RESET SIGNAL: Judy Shelton Argues Fed Cannot Ignore Metal's Multi-Year Rise
snippet: Former Trump Fed nominee Judy Shelton argues that gold's sustained multi-year advance above $4,700 is a credible monetary system distress signal the Federal Reserve is failing to acknowledge, calling for a rules-based alternative to pure discretionary policy. From 'A Gold Bug vs. Washington D.C.: Judy Shelton on the Fed, Gold, and Monetary Policy,' Money Metals, April 27, 2026.

headline: SILVER DEFIES THE BUST: After Textbook Boom-Bubble-Burst Cycle, the Metal Refuses to Crash
snippet: A GoldSeek analyst argues silver's January 2026 parabolic run followed a textbook boom-bubble-burst pattern — but the expected bust has failed to materialise, suggesting the metal has permanently revalued to a higher structural price regime driven by industrial supply deficits rather than speculative excess. April 24, 2026.

headline: FED CHAIR SHUFFLE LIFTS GOLD: DOJ Drops Powell Probe — Conventional Hawkish-Warsh Narrative Misses the Point
snippet: Gold futures closed the week at $4,738 and silver at $76.43 after the DOJ dropped its criminal probe of Fed Chair Jerome Powell, clearing the way for Kevin Warsh's confirmation. GoldSeek pushes back on the conventional 'hawkish Fed hurts gold' narrative, arguing gold's structural demand drivers are independent of who runs the Eccles Building. April 25, 2026.
